message:
fix(google-{chat,sheets,workspace-events}): apply auth headers via GoogleMaterializedAuth::apply_headers

Three sibling clients (ChatClient, SheetsClient, WorkspaceEventsClient)
shared the same broken pattern for attaching auth to outgoing HTTP
requests:

    fn bearer_token(&self) -> Option<&str> {
        match &self.auth {
            GoogleMaterializedAuth::BearerToken { access_token, .. } => Some(access_token),
            GoogleMaterializedAuth::CredentialReference { .. } => None,
        }
    }

    let token = self.bearer_token().ok_or(<Error>::Unauthorized)?;
    self.client.get(url).bearer_auth(token).send().await?

That unconditionally required a bearer token and returned
`Unauthorized` for the `CredentialReference` variant, which the
current host wiring now legitimately produces (tokens are stamped into
the `x-fcp-credential-id` header by an upstream credential-service
proxy, not carried inline). Every call through these three clients
under credential-reference auth therefore failed before hitting the
wire.

Fix: replace `bearer_token()` with a shared

    fn apply_auth_headers(&self, mut builder: RequestBuilder) -> RequestBuilder {
        let mut headers = Vec::new();
        self.auth.apply_headers(&mut headers);
        for (name, value) in headers {
            builder = builder.header(name, value);
        }
        builder
    }

which delegates to `GoogleMaterializedAuth::apply_headers` — the
shared helper that produces `Authorization: Bearer <token>` for the
bearer-token variant and the `x-fcp-credential-id: <uuid>` header for
credential-reference auth. Every `get_json` / `post_json` /
`put_json` / `delete_json` site now funnels through that one helper:

    let resp = self
        .apply_auth_headers(self.client.get(url))
        .send()
        .await
        .map_err(...)?;

Each client gains the `reqwest::RequestBuilder` import needed to spell
the helper's signature.

## Collateral

- `WorkspaceEventsClient::with_base_urls` now trims trailing slashes on
  both `events_base_url` and `pubsub_base_url`, matching the
  slash-handling its sibling connectors already did. Guarded by a new
  `with_base_urls_trims_trailing_slashes` test.
- `ChatClient` picks up a test-only `with_base_url(base_url)` seam
  (`#[cfg(test)]`, `#[must_use]`) so the new integration tests can
  point the client at a `wiremock::MockServer` without touching the
  production config surface.

## Regression coverage

Each client gets two new runtime-exercised tests (via
`fcp_async_core::runtime::block_on_sync` + `wiremock`):

- `bearer_token_requests_use_authorization_header` —
  stands up a MockServer, asserts the request carries
  `header(GOOGLE_AUTHORIZATION_HEADER, "Bearer test-token")`, and
  verifies the client succeeds for the bearer-token variant.
- `credential_reference_requests_use_fcp_credential_header` —
  mints a `fcp_core::CredentialId::new()`, asserts the outgoing
  request carries `header(FCP_CREDENTIAL_ID_HEADER,
  credential_id.to_string())`, and verifies the call succeeds under
  the variant that previously returned `Unauthorized`.

Both variants now reach the wire under both auth shapes; neither
variant is bypassable.
